summary: Market sentiment is cautiously optimistic but mixed, as progress in US-Iran nuclear and peace talks lifts geopolitical risk appetite while persistent rate concerns temper broader equity gains. The dominant macro driver is the apparent breakthrough in US-Iran diplomacy, with Secretary Rubio conducting Gulf ally consultations, Vice President Vance citing a "good foundation" for a final deal, and Pakistan's PM confirming talks concluded successfully, collectively easing fears of a wider Middle East conflict. In equity markets, mega-cap tech stocks broadly sold off dragging the S&P 500 and Nasdaq lower, with Apple standing as a notable outlier to the downside pressure, while Indian shares gained on Reliance and IT strength, and European markets remained subdued. Oil prices fell on Iran deal optimism as Hormuz tanker traffic resumed, gold ticked higher as a residual safe-haven hedge, the dollar edged up post-talks, and SpaceX drew attention by launching a bond sale while disclosing a cash pile exceeding $100 billion. The primary risks investors are monitoring include the durability of the Iran agreement and whether a final deal materializes, the trajectory of interest rates which are offsetting geopolitical relief, and residual energy market volatility, with the short-term outlook pointing to range-bound, event-driven trading contingent on further diplomatic confirmation and incoming central bank signals.
